prompt:
The reports integration host no longer boots after our ASP.NET Core upgrade. Building PortalHost reaches the security middleware and throws that required authorization services were never registered; the service collection still contains a legacy snapshot that the current host does not consume. Protected local migration notes describe the supported registration and pipeline contract.

Please migrate the authentication and authorization wiring while preserving behavior. PortalHeader remains the default authenticate and challenge scheme, with the existing 401 challenge. `reports.read` requires an authenticated portal user with that scope and returns 403 for an authenticated user who lacks it. `operations` must select only ServiceKey, accept the operations identity, and challenge through ServiceKey when another scheme is presented. Keep authentication before authorization and ensure the integration host starts and stops through its injected no-socket server.

Run `dotnet test`. The xUnit suite, project, ignore file, and migration notes are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: migrate ASP.NET authentication and authorization service and middleware registration while preserving multi-scheme selection and integration-host behavior
full_distill.baseline_evidence: dotnet test deterministically fails because PortalHost.Build reaches UseAuthorization without current authorization services and direct scheme lookup cannot resolve IAuthenticationSchemeProvider from the legacy snapshot
full_distill.success_contract: the injected-server host starts offline, PortalHeader remains the default report authentication and challenge scheme, report scope denial is a forbid, ServiceKey exclusively owns operations authorization, and the middleware order authenticates before authorizing
adversarial_factors: a protected PortalHost source-order probe requires the single UseAuthentication call to precede the single UseAuthorization call, so a registration-only fix cannot leave the reversed pipeline behind
an authenticated portal user lacking reports.read must receive 403 without a challenge, rejecting policies that collapse authentication and scope authorization
the operations endpoint is tried with both ServiceKey and a valid PortalHeader identity, proving its policy selects a scheme instead of accepting any authenticated principal
default scheme provider assertions and exact per-scheme WWW-Authenticate values reject a superficial policy-only fix or one shared challenge handler
the capture server asserts start and stop counts and exposes no addresses, so bypassing host startup or opening a live listener cannot satisfy integration acceptance
